subject: Geology
title: Igneous Textures
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Igneous Textures.

hook.text: A rock's texture tells you exactly how it was born. Large crystals mean slow cooling. No crystals mean instant.

buildup.text: Igneous texture describes crystal size: coarse-grained, fine-grained, glassy, or porphyritic (mixed sizes).

discovery.text: Porphyritic texture means the magma cooled in two stages: slowly at depth, then quickly at the surface.

twist.text: Pegmatites have enormous crystals, some meters long. They form from water-rich magma that cooled very slowly.

climax.text: Texture is a window into a rock's past. Without cutting or testing, you can read its cooling history at a glance.

punchline.text: Crystal size tells the cooling story.

quiz.question: What does porphyritic texture indicate?
options: The magma cooled in two stages at different rates
